complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the project setup into: (1) Configure project.godot display and engine settings, (2) Create and configure input action map with all movement and UI actions, (3) Create folder structure (scenes/, scripts/, assets/), (4) Implement Main.tscn with scene router script and test scene switching logic
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Low complexity greenfield task. The project.godot file exists with basic config. Task involves straightforward configuration editing and creating a simple scene router pattern. Input map creation is UI-based with clear requirements. No existing code to refactor or dependencies to manage. Main risk is ensuring Main.tscn is set as the main scene in project settings.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide static rendering into: (1) Create Playfield.gd with grid constants and _draw() method for background and grid lines, (2) Create Game.tscn with Node2D root and Playfield child node, (3) Implement Snake.gd with static body array and _draw() rendering, (4) Implement Apple.gd with static position and _draw() rendering, (5) Configure Camera2D positioning and test complete scene rendering
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Low-medium complexity. Requires understanding Godot's node system, scene structure, and 2D drawing API. Multiple scripts need to be created (Playfield.gd, Snake.gd, Apple.gd, Game.gd) but all with simple static rendering. The _draw() function and queue_redraw() pattern may be unfamiliar. Camera2D setup is straightforward. No complex logic, just coordinate math and rendering calls.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break movement system into: (1) Add Timer node to Game.tscn and configure tick rate properties, (2) Update Snake.gd to add heading vector and move() method with body manipulation, (3) Implement Game.gd signal connection between Timer.timeout and snake movement, (4) Test tick-based movement and verify snake maintains constant length while moving
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium complexity. Introduces game loop timing with Timer node and signal system. Requires understanding array manipulation (insert at head, pop from tail) to create movement effect. The heading vector pattern and queue_redraw() after movement are key concepts. No collision detection yet keeps it manageable. Main challenge is coordinating Timer signals with snake state updates.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide input system into: (1) Add input queueing variables (heading, queued_heading) to Snake.gd, (2) Implement queue_direction() method with 180-degree reversal rejection logic, (3) Update Snake.move() to apply queued heading at tick boundaries, (4) Add _process() input polling in Game.gd for all four directions, (5) Test edge cases: 180-degree reversal blocking, rapid multi-input between ticks, and input-tick synchronization
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium complexity with important edge case handling. The queued input pattern prevents illegal moves and handles rapid inputs between ticks - critical for good snake game feel. The 180-degree reversal check (new_direction + heading != Vector2i.ZERO) is elegant but requires careful testing. Must coordinate _process() (runs every frame) with tick-based movement. Input action names must match Task 1's setup.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break growth mechanics into: (1) Implement Apple.respawn() method with empty cell calculation logic avoiding snake body, (2) Add pending_growth counter and grow() method to Snake.gd, (3) Update Snake.move() to conditionally skip tail removal based on pending_growth, (4) Add collision detection in Game._on_game_tick() to check head position against apple, (5) Test growth behavior, apple respawn randomness, and edge case where grid is nearly full
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium complexity with algorithmic challenge. The respawn logic must iterate all 400 grid cells, exclude snake body positions, and randomly select from remaining cells - O(n²) operation but acceptable for 20x20 grid. Growth implementation via pending_growth counter and conditional tail removal is clean but requires understanding the deferred growth pattern. Edge case: what happens when snake fills most of grid?

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Collision Detection and Game Over State
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide collision system into: (1) Implement Snake.check_collision() with wall boundary checking using grid constants, (2) Add self-collision detection looping through body segments, (3) Add is_game_over state flag and game_over() method to Game.gd, (4) Update _on_game_tick() to check collisions before apple eating, (5) Implement restart_game() using get_tree().reload_current_scene(), (6) Update _process() to handle game over input blocking and restart input detection
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium-high complexity due to state management. Collision detection logic itself is straightforward (boundary checks and loop for self-collision), but properly handling game over state affects multiple systems: input processing, tick handling, and scene reloading. Must ensure Timer stops on game over to prevent further ticks. The restart mechanism using scene reload is simple but requires understanding Godot's scene tree API. Careful ordering of collision checks matters (wall/self before apple).

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break HUD implementation into: (1) Add CanvasLayer and Label nodes to Game.tscn scene tree, (2) Configure ScoreLabel properties (position, font size, color theming), (3) Add score variable and update_hud() method to Game.gd, (4) Integrate score increment in apple eating logic and test HUD updates during gameplay and restart
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Low-medium complexity UI task. CanvasLayer ensures HUD renders on top of game elements. Label configuration is mostly UI properties. The update_hud() pattern with string concatenation is simple. Integration point is clear (increment score when apple eaten). Main consideration is label visibility against dark background - may need theme overrides or background panel. Scene reload resets score automatically.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide persistence system into: (1) Create HighScore.gd autoload singleton script with high_score variable and SAVE_PATH constant, (2) Implement load_high_score() using FileAccess API with error handling for missing files, (3) Implement save_high_score() with comparison logic and file writing, (4) Register HighScore as autoload in project settings, (5) Integrate HighScore.save_high_score() into Game.game_over() and test persistence across launches
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium complexity due to file I/O and autoload pattern. FileAccess API in Godot 4 differs from Godot 3 (no File.new(), uses static methods). Must handle missing file case gracefully on first launch. The autoload/singleton pattern may be new concept. File path user:// maps to OS-specific locations. Simple text file format (storing single integer as string) keeps it manageable. Testing requires closing and relaunching Godot, not just scene reload.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break menu creation into: (1) Create MainMenu.tscn with Control root and VBoxContainer layout structure, (2) Add and configure all UI elements (title label, high score label, buttons) with theme properties, (3) Implement MainMenu.gd with _ready() to display HighScore data and setup button signals, (4) Implement scene transition methods (_on_start_pressed, _on_quit_pressed) using scene tree API, (5) Test menu flow: launch to menu, start game, game over, return to menu with updated high score
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium complexity UI scene creation. Requires understanding Control nodes, container layouts, and anchor presets for responsive centering. Button signal connections and focus management for keyboard navigation. Scene transitions use get_tree().change_scene_to_file() which differs from change_scene() method. Must ensure Main.tscn (or project settings) launches MainMenu.tscn, not Game.tscn directly. High score integration tests HighScore autoload from Task 8.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Game Over Screen and Pause Overlay
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide overlay systems into: (1) Create GameOverOverlay CanvasLayer with semi-transparent ColorRect and VBoxContainer in Game.tscn, (2) Add all game over UI elements including conditional 'NEW BEST!' label, (3) Create PauseOverlay CanvasLayer with PROCESS_MODE_WHEN_PAUSED and pause UI, (4) Implement toggle_pause() using get_tree().paused and overlay visibility, (5) Enhance game_over() to compare old/new high scores and show_game_over_screen(), (6) Set process_mode properties on all game nodes for proper pause behavior, (7) Test pause during gameplay, game over states (new high score vs not), and navigation between overlays/scenes
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium-high complexity due to multiple overlays and pause system. Godot's pause system requires understanding process_mode settings (PAUSABLE vs WHEN_PAUSED) on nodes. Game over overlay has conditional logic (show 'NEW BEST!' only when high score beaten) requiring old vs new comparison before HighScore.save_high_score(). Button signal connections must be set up in show_game_over_screen(). ColorRect semi-transparency for overlays needs color with alpha. Managing three states: playing, paused, game over with proper input blocking.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break polish into: (1) Update Snake._draw() to add distinct head rendering with darker color and 1px padding, (2) Implement directional eye rendering on snake head based on heading vector, (3) Refine Playfield color scheme (background, grid lines, border) for better aesthetics, (4) Apply consistent color palette to Apple and Snake body segments, (5) Create unified theme/style for all UI text elements (font, size, shadows/outlines), (6) Add HUD background panel and ensure consistent styling across all overlays (menu, pause, game over)
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium complexity artistic/polish task. Snake head eyes require different draw_circle() calls based on heading direction (4 cases to implement). The 1px padding in Rect2 calculations (cell * CELL_SIZE + 1, CELL_SIZE - 2) affects all rendering. Color consistency across multiple scripts (Playfield.gd, Snake.gd, Apple.gd) and UI scenes. Theme creation or theme overrides for consistent text styling. Subjective visual quality makes testing harder - requires iteration and visual inspection rather than functional tests.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide export process into: (1) Configure Windows Desktop export preset with all settings (name, icon, embed PCK, architecture), (2) Create builds/windows/ directory structure and export executable, (3) Test exported build on development machine with full game loop and persistence verification, (4) Verify user data directory location and highscore.save file creation in exported build, (5) Document export settings and test on clean Windows machine without Godot to ensure independence
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Medium complexity deployment task. Export preset configuration is mostly UI-driven in Godot editor. Embedded PCK simplifies distribution. Testing persistence in exported build requires understanding user:// path maps differently in exported vs editor (OS.get_user_data_dir()). Clean machine testing is important but may not be feasible without additional hardware/VM. Icon creation (256x256) may require external tool. File size expectations and debug vs release builds. Less about code, more about build configuration and testing methodology.
